title: Independent-race score-space stack with sigmoid calibration only on OOF predictions
summary: Calibration itself should not create AUC lift unless it changes ranks, but stacking heterogeneous models can.
rationale: Calibration itself should not create AUC lift unless it changes ranks, but stacking heterogeneous models can. Scikit-learn notes calibrators must be fit on independent data; sigmoid preserves ranking, while isotonic can create ties and change AUC. This matters if you combine binary GBDT, AFT-derived risk, and ranker scores.
implementation_hint: Build fold-local OOF predictions from the current binary model, the AFT model, the ranker, and CatBoost. Train a simple meta-learner on those OOF scores using strict race-blocked folds. If probability calibration is needed, fit only a sigmoid calibrator on the stack’s OOF scores, never on in-fold predictions.
expected_effect: More stable leaderboard transfer than averaging raw probabilities from misaligned model families; likely modest but real gain if model errors are complementary.
risk: If the base models are too correlated, the stack adds noise. Avoid isotonic unless you verify it does not hurt AUC via ties.
